title: Sec. 47-512. - Cleaning and maintenance requirements. (Houston Code of Ordinances, Ch. 47, Art. XI, Div. 9, Subdiv. 1)
excerpt: (b) Each interceptor or holding tank shall be fully evacuated at least once every 90 days. (c) ... more frequently than once every 90 days when: (1) Twenty-five percent or more of the wetted height of the grease trap or interceptor, as measured from the bottom of the device to the invert of the outlet pipe, contains floating materials, sediment, oils or greases; ... (f) In any event, a grease trap or holding tank shall be fully evacuated, cleaned, inspected, and, if needed, repaired or otherwise maintained, at least once every 180 days. (Ord. No. 97-196; ... Ord. No. 2020-1024, 12-2-2020)
query: library.municode.com Houston grease trap interceptor pretreatment ordinance

title: Chapter 47 - WATER AND SEWERS, Article XI - Transportation and Treatment of Certain Wastes, Division 9 (Secs. 47-511 to 47-514) - Houston Code of Ordinances
excerpt: Sec. 47-513 Interceptor requirement: Commercial, institutional, and industrial facilities including restaurants, cafeterias, bars... shall design, install, operate and maintain an interceptor complying with the Plumbing Code and install a sample well. Sec. 47-514 Discharge parameters: unlawful to discharge FOG exceeding average of 200 mg/L of free or emulsified fats, oils or grease.
query: library.municode.com Houston grease trap interceptor pretreatment ordinance

title: Houston Code of Ordinances, Ch. 47, Art. XI, Div. 9, Sec. 47-512 - Cleaning and maintenance requirements
excerpt: (b) Each interceptor or holding tank shall be fully evacuated at least once every 90 days. (c) ... more frequently than once every 90 days when: (1) Twenty-five percent or more of the wetted height of the grease trap or interceptor, as measured from the bottom of the device to the invert of the outlet pipe, contains floating materials, sediment, oils or greases ... (f) In any event, a grease trap or holding tank shall be fully evacuated, cleaned, inspected ... at least once every 180 days.

title: Dallas Water Utilities Pretreatment Program FAQ (grease trap cleaning)
excerpt: The Dallas City Code, Chapter 19, Sec 19-126.2 (c) specifies all grease traps must be completely emptied and cleaned by a licensed hauler. ... The interceptors must be cleaned as often as necessary but not less than every 90 days, or whenever 25% or more of the interceptor contains floating materials, sediment, oils, or grease.

title: SAWS FOG Ordinance (2011-05-12-0378), San Antonio City Code Ch. 34, Div. 5, Sec. 34-527 Maintenance
excerpt: Sec. 34-527 (b) Frequency. ... shall evacuate accumulated solids, fats, oils, grease and all other material(s) from each interceptor at a frequency not less often than every ninety (90) days and within two (2) working days whenever twenty-five (25) percent or more of the wetted height of the interceptor, measured from the bottom of the device to the invert of the outlet pipe, contains floating materials, sediment, fats, oils, or grease.

title: Austin Water - Grease Trap Maintenance (Austin City Code Ch. 15-10, Sec. 15-10-197)
excerpt: Grease traps must be pumped out at least once every 90 days, or sooner if grease and solids make up 50% or more of the wetted height of the trap as measured from the bottom of the trap to the invert of the outlet pipe (§ 15-10-197).

title: Austin Water - Pretreatment Frequently Asked Questions (§15-10-197)
excerpt: Austin City Code §15-10-197 requires all grease traps to be completely emptied and cleaned by a permitted liquid waste hauler at least once every three months - or more frequently if grease and solids in the final compartment accumulate to 50% or more of the wetted height before three months have passed.

title: SAWS Grease Reduction Program: FOG Ordinance (program slides)
excerpt: What is the Standard? Wetted height of the interceptor: 25% or more - Measured from bottom of the device to invert of outlet pipe - Contains floating materials, sediment, fats, oils, or grease ... Sec. 34-527 Maintenance.
